## Headline counts

| _Total_ | 1715 |

### Status definitions

- **No change** — researcher/AI confirms the verse is correctly placed; no edit needed. Not auto-assigned.
- **Ready with changes** — clear new_vcg proposal, sub-group aligned, no conflict flags.
- **For Review** — proposal exists but carries one or more flags: `subgroup_fallback`, `multi_candidate`, `ai_annotation`, `meaning_vcg_mismatch`.
- **For research** — missing critical info: no new_vcg AND/OR no meaning, duplicate vr_id, status P/UT, or no current group_id.

_Read each verse card below to confirm or override the status._

`[132798]` · **1Sa 4:20** · `H7896K shit` (to set: consider) · _G_ · flags: ai_annotation

### M15-G — 3 verses

`[44127]` · **2Cor 2:11** · `G3540 noēma` (mind/thought) · _G_ · flags: ai_annotation

> **Verse:** 2Cor 2:11 so that we would not be outwitted by Satan ; for we are not ignorant of his designs .
> **Meaning:** noēma names Satan's designs or thought-schemes — the formed inner strategy of the adversary that the apostolic community knows and guards against.
> **AI annotation:** `[Satan's]`
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._
> **New SG**     `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `1188-001` — _Term names the inner faculty of thought — the mind's contents and its susceptibility to hardening, blinding, or corrupting by spiritual forces_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-07` — The inner cognitive faculty as site of spiritual contest: noēma — _The mind as inner cognitive faculty is not neutral — it can be hardened, blinded, led astray, or guarded. Satan works through it; God guards it; the apostolic mission takes its thoughts captive. All six uses locate noēma as a contested inner terrain._

> **New VCG**     `VCG-B-05` — Intimate teaching-to-walk: tir.gal as tender, unrecognised nurture — _A single verse. God teaching Ephraim to walk — the intimate relational dynamic of the caring parent taking up the child's arms. The pathos is that the one taught did not know who healed them._

`[180977]` · **Mat 1:23** · `G3177 methermēneuō` (to mean) · _G_

> **Verse:** Mat 1:23 “ Behold , the virgin shall conceive and bear a son , and they shall call his name Immanuel ” ( which means , God with us ).
> **Meaning:** methermēneuō names the translation that reveals the inner theological meaning of the name — the act of translating Immanuel discloses that the name carries "God with us" as its inner content. Translation as theological revelation.

> **New SG**     `M15-B` — Understanding as inner perceptive faculty ⚠️ change — _Terms that name the inner faculty of understanding — the capacity to receive, perceive, and grasp what is being revealed or communicated. Morally conditioned: the faculty is enabled by humility before God and blocked by…_
> **Current VCG** `973-001` — _Term names translation that reveals the theological heart of a name — Immanuel as the inner meaning of God's incarnate presence_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-B-06` — Translation revealing the inner theological meaning of a name: methermēneuō — _The act of translation that discloses what a name means at its theological core — Immanuel meaning God-with-us._

> **New VCG**     `VCG-A-12` — se.khel: discernment as personal inner quality producing good outcomes — _A distinctive inner quality of practical wisdom — discernment, good sense, shrewd perception — that produces favourable outcomes and is recognisable in persons._

> **Verse:** Est 6:13 And Haman told his wife Zeresh and all his friends everything that had happened to him. Then his wise men and his wife Zeresh said to him, “ If Mordecai , before whom you have begun to fall , is of the Jewish people , you will not overcome him but will surely fall before him.”
> **Meaning:** cha.kham names Haman's wise men as his advisors — but their wisdom here delivers a judgment of doom against him. Their counsel is accurate but unwelcome.
> **Current SG** `M15-A` — Wisdom as holistic inner character and orientation — _Terms that name wisdom as a constituted holistic quality of the inner person — a settled orientation of the whole person that shapes perception, decision, and character. Includes the structural opposite (unwise). Spirit-…_
> **New SG**     `M15-A` — Wisdom as holistic inner character and orientation ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `528-005` — _cha.kham here names wisdom as the qualification for a role — the wise man sought for appointment, the wise men of the court, those who have the inner capacity for governance, law, and counsel. The focus is on wisdom as a capacity that qualifies persons for ins…_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-A-03` — Professional court wise men as institutional class: cha.kham / chak.kim as class title — _The court class of professional wise men — summoned for interpretation, giving counsel, named as the foil for what Spirit-given wisdom can do. Their title and incapacity stand together in the Daniel narratives._

> **New VCG**     `VCG-A-05` — Self-attributed wisdom: cha.kham as false inner claim — _Persons who attribute wisdom to themselves without having it — the inner claim is exposed as false, hollow, or dangerous._

> **New VCG**     `VCG-C-07` — ya.da as the "then you shall know" pattern: divine self-disclosure through event producing recognition — _A repeated pattern across the OT: God acts, and the purpose of the action is that persons will know — know that he is the Lord, that he is present, that he sent his messenger. The knowing is produced by the event, not by the seeker._

`[178159]` · **Pro 10:14** · `H1847 da.at` (knowledge) · _G_

> **Verse:** Pro 10:14 The wise lay up knowledge , but the mouth of a fool brings ruin near .
> **Meaning:** da.at names knowledge as something accumulated and stored inwardly by the wise — an inner treasury.
> **Current SG** `M15-C` — Knowledge as inner content and covenantal knowing — _Terms that name knowledge as both inner content held in the knowing self and the constitutive relational orientation of knowing God. Includes the covenantal knowing of God and its structural opposite (spiritual ignorance…_
> **New SG**     `M15-C` — Knowledge as inner content and covenantal knowing ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `955-001` — _Knowledge as the foundational inner capacity — rooted in the fear of the Lord (Pro 1:7) and experienced inwardly as delight in the soul (Pro 2:10); the cognitive-spiritual capacity that grounds wisdom._
> **New VCG**     `VCG-C-01` — da.at as inner possession: knowledge as an inner quality of the wise person — _Knowledge as what the wise person holds inwardly — the inner capacity/content that Proverbs aims to produce, that the wise accumulate and guard, and that gives the person strength._

### M15-D — 118 verses

`[50886]` · **1Cor 11:29** · `G1252 diakrinō` (to judge/doubt) · _G_

> **Verse:** 1Cor 11:29 For anyone who eats and drinks without discerning the body eats and drinks judgment on himself .
> **Meaning:** diakrinō names the inner discerning act of recognising what the body of Christ is — failure to make this distinction in the Lord's Supper produces self-judgment.
> **Current SG** `M15-D` — Discernment and practical judgment — _Terms that name the applied perceptive act — judging situations wisely, making right moral-practical distinctions, and the conscience-adjacent evaluation of thoughts. Includes directionally neutral terms (same inner qual…_
> **New SG**     `M15-D` — Discernment and practical judgment ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `1285-001` — _Term names the inner act of judging and distinguishing — morally discerning between persons or things, including wrongful discrimination_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-D-08` — diakrinō as discriminating judgment: inner act of making evaluative distinctions between persons or matters — _The inner act of judging and distinguishing — discerning between persons, weighing speech, making or not-making discriminations. The judgment can be right (weighing prophecy, discerning the Lord's body) or wrong (partiality, unjust criticism)._

`[148494]` · **1Ch 13:1** · `H3289 ya.ats` (to advise) · _G_

> **Verse:** 1Ch 13:1 David consulted with the commanders of thousands and of hundreds , with every leader .
> **Meaning:** ya.ats names David's broad consultation before a major decision — purposive wisdom gathered from many sources.
> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._
> **New SG**     `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `749-001` — _Counsel as wisdom given or received between persons, shaping inner orientation_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-01` — Human counsel: wisdom given between persons shaping deliberation — _One person gives advice or counsel to another — the inner wisdom of the advisor expressed and offered to shape the recipient's purposes. The outcome depends on whether counsel is received or rejected._

`[131754]` · **2Cor 1:17** · `G1011 bouleuō` (to plan) · _G_

> **Verse:** 2Cor 1:17 Was I vacillating when I wanted to do this ? Do I make my plans according to the flesh , ready to say “ Yes , yes ” and “ No , no ” at the same time?

> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._

> **Current VCG** `749-001` — _Counsel as wisdom given or received between persons, shaping inner orientation_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-01` — Human counsel: wisdom given between persons shaping deliberation — _One person gives advice or counsel to another — the inner wisdom of the advisor expressed and offered to shape the recipient's purposes. The outcome depends on whether counsel is received or rejected._

`[131758]` · **Joh 11:53** · `G1011 bouleuō` (to plan) · _G_

> **Verse:** Joh 11:53 So from that day on they made plans to put him to death .
> **Meaning:** bouleuō names the deliberate inner resolve formed against Jesus — the council's purposive decision to kill him, made and held from that moment.
> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._

> **Current VCG** `2109-002` — _Term names deliberate planning directed toward harm — the inner will forming an intention against another_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-12` — Deliberate resolve to harm: bouleuō naming purposive decision directed against another — _The inner will resolving upon a course of action specifically directed toward harming or killing another person._

`[131759]` · **Joh 12:10** · `G1011 bouleuō` (to plan) · _G_

> **Verse:** Joh 12:10 So the chief priests made plans to put Lazarus to death as well ,
> **Meaning:** bouleuō names the extended deliberate resolve — the same purposive intent extended to include Lazarus because his existence was evidence for Jesus.
> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._

> **Current VCG** `2109-002` — _Term names deliberate planning directed toward harm — the inner will forming an intention against another_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-12` — Deliberate resolve to harm: bouleuō naming purposive decision directed against another — _The inner will resolving upon a course of action specifically directed toward harming or killing another person._

`[103351]` · **Jon 1:6** · `H6245B a.shat` (to think) · _G_

> **Verse:** Jon 1:6 So the captain came and said to him, “ What do you mean, you sleeper ? Arise , call out to your god ! Perhaps the god will give a thought to us, that we may not perish .”
> **Meaning:** a.shat names the hoped-for divine inner attending — the captain appeals for the god to direct thought toward the crew in their danger. The appeal is for purposive divine attention to their need.
> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._
> **New SG**     `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `3445-001` — _The hope and cry that God would direct his thought toward persons in their need — the inner appeal for divine attention as a response to crisis (Jon 1:6)._
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-18` — The appeal for divine purposive attending: a.shat as hope for divine attention in crisis — _A single verse. The captain appeals for the god to give thought to the crew — the hope that divine inner purposing will be directed toward those in need of rescue._

`[15332]` · **Judg 19:30** · `H5779 uts` (to plan) · _G_

> **Verse:** Judg 19:30 And all who saw it said , “ Such a thing has never happened or been seen from the day that the people of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt until this day ; consider it, take counsel , and speak .”
> **Meaning:** uts names the deliberate communal inner purposing — the people are called to gather their collective will in response to what has happened.
> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._
> **New SG**     `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `754-001` — _Deliberate inner purposing — planning together in response to a significant situation_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-21` — Communal deliberate purposing: uts as collective counsel-forming — _The community deliberately gathering its collective will to form a shared purpose in response to a significant situation._

`[21545]` · **Lam 2:17** · `H2161 za.mam` (to plan) · _G_

> **Verse:** Lam 2:17 The Lord has done what he purposed ; he has carried out his word , which he commanded long ago ; he has thrown down without pity ; he has made the enemy rejoice over you and exalted the might of your foes .
> **Meaning:** za.mam names God's resolute ancient purposing fulfilled — what was formed as intention long ago has now been enacted.
> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._
> **New SG**     `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `847-002` — _Term names resolute divine purposing — intention formed and carried through without relenting_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-04` — Divine counsel: God's sovereign purposive intent directed toward nations and history — _God forms and executes sovereign intention that cannot be annulled or redirected. His counsel is self-originating, irresistible, and encompasses the whole of history. Multiple human terms (ya.ats, cha.shav, za.mam) converge in expressing this._

`[130788]` · **Lam 2:8** · `H2803I cha.shav` (to devise: devise) · _G_

> **Verse:** Lam 2:8 The Lord determined to lay in ruins the wall of the daughter of Zion ; he stretched out the measuring line ; he did not restrain his hand from destroying ; he caused rampart and wall to lament ; they languished together .
> **Meaning:** cha.shav names God's purposive determination of judgment — a deliberate, formed intention of destruction. The verb is divine and intentional, not reluctant.
> **Current SG** `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent — _Terms that name the inner capacity of purposive forward-orientation — forming intent, devising plans, giving and receiving counsel, making deliberate decisions. Includes divine and human purposive activity._
> **New SG**     `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `3334-002` — _Term names purposive planning — the inner formation of a deliberate purpose or plan, whether by God or human agents_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-05` — Divine purposive planning with conditional openness to relenting — _God forms purposive intent that is held open — the plan is real and announced, but its execution is conditional on human response. This is different from VCG-E-04 (irresistible) — here the divine intent can be withdrawn if the people turn._

`[131760]` · **Luk 14:31** · `G1011 bouleuō` (to plan) · _G_

> **Verse:** Luk 14:31 Or what king , going out to encounter another king in war , will not sit down first and deliberate whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand ?

> **New SG**     `M15-E` — Deliberative planning, counsel, and purposive intent ✅ same

> **New VCG**     `VCG-E-11` — Deliberate inner decision-making: bouleuō as the mind resolving upon a course of action — _The inner weighing of options leading to a resolved decision — the mind deliberating and settling upon a course of action._

### M15-G — 24 verses

`[128665]` · **1Cor 14:20** · `G5424 frēn` (thinking) · _G_

> **Verse:** 1Cor 14:20 Brothers , do not be children in your thinking . Be infants in evil , but in your thinking be mature .
> **Meaning:** frēn names the faculty of mature thinking — the capacity for rational and moral discernment that marks adult inner life. To be children in thinking is to be undeveloped in the faculty; to be mature is to have it trained and functioning at full capacity.
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._
> **New SG**     `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `2493-001` — _Term names the faculty of mature thinking — the capacity for moral and rational discernment that marks adult inner life_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-08` — The faculty of mature thinking: frēn — _A single verse. The capacity for moral and rational discernment — named as a faculty that can be childlike or mature. The command is to be mature in this faculty while being infants in evil._

`[128756]` · **1Pe 4:1** · `G1771 ennoia` (thought/purpose) · _G_

> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._
> **New SG**     `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `3372-001` — _Term names the inner thoughts and purposes of the heart — the hidden intentional life that only God's word can discern and expose_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-09` — Inner thoughts and purposes of the heart; the way of thinking to be adopted: ennoia — _The inner thought-content and purposive orientation of the heart — what only God's word can discern (Heb 4:12), and the way of thinking to be taken on as armour (1Pe 4:1)._

`[178462]` · **2Ch 1:10** · `H4093 mad.da` (knowledge) · _G_

The knowledge is requested, given by God, and enables wise leadership._

`[178463]` · **2Ch 1:11** · `H4093 mad.da` (knowledge) · _G_

`[44125]` · **2Cor 10:5** · `G3540 noēma` (mind/thought) · _G_

> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._

`[103389]` · **Dan 2:29** · `H7476 ra.yon` (thought) · _G_

> **Verse:** Dan 2:29 To you , O king , as you lay in bed came thoughts of what would be after this , and he who reveals mysteries made known to you what is to be .
> **Meaning:** ra.yon names Nebuchadnezzar's inner thoughts arising in the night — the formed thought-content produced by the king's mind in sleep, which produced the dream.
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._

> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-03` — Thoughts alarmed by revelatory dream-visions: ra.yon — _Inner thought-content produced in response to divine revelation through dreams or visions — in every case the ra.yon disturbs or alarms the person receiving or hearing of it. Includes the thoughts the queen counsels the king not to allow to master him._

`[178465]` · **Ecc 10:20** · `H4093 mad.da` (knowledge) · _G_

> **Verse:** Ecc 10:20 Even in your thoughts , do not curse the king , nor in your bedroom curse the rich , for a bird of the air will carry your voice , or some winged creature tell the matter .
> **Meaning:** mad.da names the inner thought-life or consciousness as the moral seat — even private thoughts carry moral accountability. The word serves here as a reference to inner conscious life.
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._
> **New SG**     `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `960-002` — _Thought-life or inner consciousness as moral seat of the person_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-02` — Inner consciousness as the moral seat: mad.da as thought-life — _Mad.da used for the thought-life or inner consciousness as the place where private moral evaluation occurs — even private thoughts carry accountability._

`[43589]` · **Job 12:5** · `H6248 ash.tut` (thought) · _G_

> **Verse:** Job 12:5 In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune ; it is ready for those whose feet slip .
> **Meaning:** ash.tut names the inner thought or attitude of the comfortable person — the formed inner disposition of those at ease, from which contempt for the suffering arises. Ease-induced inner contempt.
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._

> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-05` — Ease-induced inner contempt for suffering: ash.tut — _A single verse. The inner attitude of those who are comfortable — from their ease, they form inner contempt for those whose feet slip into misfortune._

`[101360]` · **Phili 4:7** · `G3540 noēma` (mind/thought) · _G_

> **Verse:** Phili 4:7 And the peace of God , which surpasses all understanding , will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus .
> **Meaning:** noēma names the minds that God's peace will guard — the inner cognitive faculty held secure by divine peace in Christ.
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._
> **New SG**     `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `1188-001` — _Term names the inner faculty of thought — the mind's contents and its susceptibility to hardening, blinding, or corrupting by spiritual forces_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-07` — The inner cognitive faculty as site of spiritual contest: noēma — _The mind as inner cognitive faculty is not neutral — it can be hardened, blinded, led astray, or guarded. `[103401]` · **Psa 139:17** · `H7454 re.a` (thought) · _G_

> **Verse:** Psa 139:17 How precious to me are your thoughts , O God ! How vast is the sum of them!
> **Meaning:** re.a names God's thoughts as the object of the psalmist's wondering — divine inner thought-content is precious and vast beyond counting. The same word that named the psalmist's thoughts known by God is now applied to God's own thoughts that are precious to the psalmist.
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._
> **New SG**     `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `3453-001` — _Thoughts known to and held by God — the inner thoughts of persons discerned by God from afar, and God's own thoughts precious to the psalmist_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-04` — Inner thoughts known by God and precious to the psalmist: re.a — _The psalmist's thoughts are transparent to God, discerned from afar. God's own thoughts are precious and vast beyond counting. The same word applies in both directions, creating a symmetry of mutual thought-content._

`[103328]` · **Psa 146:4** · `H6250 esh.to.nah` (thought) · _G_

> **Verse:** Psa 146:4 When his breath departs , he returns to the earth ; on that very day his plans perish .
> **Meaning:** esh.to.nah names the plans that cease at death — the formed inner thoughts and purposes that belong to the living person and vanish the moment life ends.
> **Current SG** `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts — _Terms that name the formed thoughts held within the mind — inner content as objects held, which can be guarded, captured, led astray, or disclosed. Static (distinct from reflective activity)._
> **New SG**     `M15-G` — Inner thought-content — the mind's formed thoughts ✅ same
> **Current VCG** `3442-001` — _Plans that perish at death — the inner thoughts and purposes that cease when the person dies, disclosing the fragility of inner purposing_
> **New VCG**     `VCG-G-06` — Plans and thoughts that perish at death: esh.to.nah as mortality marker — _A single verse. The inner purposings and plans of a person belong to the living — on the day their breath departs, these plans perish. Inner thought-content is constitutively linked to life._
